title: AI Model Assessment
public_claim: AI Model Assessment is presented as an AI assurance and retrieval-integrity instrument.
distinctive_mechanism: The working mechanism is to move from threat model to test condition, system behavior, error mode, human-review checkpoint, scoring rule, and release or rejection gate. The reader should be able to ask what enters the room, what transformation occurs, what judgment or work product leaves, and which gate prevents overclaiming.

Verify that the component produces: a project-specific work product, decision, or proof surface tied to `Add Method`

Preserve the evidence at or beside: separate prototype capability from validated finding, bind claims to test materials, expose false-positive and false-negative risks, and add method-specific source anchors. The project source notes below should be.
acceptance_criteria: The input, operation, and expected output are all visible.
The evidence locator is present or the missing evidence is explicitly named.
The result supports the component claim without implying broader completion.
component_demo_cases.claim_ceiling: The page may claim proof-structured components and visible verification targets. It may not claim external validation, production deployment, or completed empirical findings unless the project-specific record states those facts.
component_demo_cases.next_build_task: Confirm `Add Method` appears in the cited source context and that the public page explains what output, decision, or project function it supports.
